disclaimer: Entry rules can change. Always verify with official immigration or embassy sources before booking.

visa.notes: US passport holders are entitled to a 30-day visa-free tourist stay in the Philippines. No visa is required on arrival. A return or onward ticket is generally required and may be checked at check-in or immigration. Passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond your intended departure date.
visa.verify_note: Philippines immigration rules can change. Verify current visa exemption terms at the Bureau of Immigration Philippines or via the US Embassy in Manila before booking.

insurance.notes: Not legally required but strongly recommended. Ensure your policy covers: scuba diving and water activities (often excluded by default), medical evacuation from remote islands, and adventure activities. Hospital costs for serious incidents can reach $12,000 or more outside of Manila and Cebu.

regions.notes: Peak dry season Dec–Apr: sunny and ideal for beaches and Palawan. Heavy typhoon risk Jun–Oct, especially in and around Manila and northern Luzon.
Drier and less typhoon-prone than Luzon. Dec–May is excellent for diving, beaches, and Boracay. Seas get rough Jul–Sep.
Less affected by typhoons than northern Philippines. Siargao surf season peaks Aug–Nov. Davao is dry Mar–May. Check local conditions by sub-region before travel.

budget.verify_note: Rates are approximate. Check xe.com before travel.

budget.label: Backpacker
budget.includes: Dorm bed, turo-turo meals, local transport, street snacks

mid.label: Comfort
mid.includes: Private guesthouse, local restaurants, Grab rides, activities

high.label: Boutique
high.includes: Boutique hotel, dining out, island tours, scuba diving

useful_apps.name: Google Maps
useful_apps.use: Navigation — download offline maps before arriving

useful_apps.name: Klook
useful_apps.use: Book island tours, activities, and transport

useful_apps.name: Agoda
useful_apps.use: Accommodation — competitive rates across the Philippines

useful_apps.name: Airalo
useful_apps.use: eSIM data — buy before you land

useful_apps.name: 12Go
useful_apps.use: Ferry and transport booking between islands

transport: Domestic flights are the fastest way to travel between major islands — book early on Cebu Pacific or Philippine Airlines for the best fares.
Ferries connect most islands but vary heavily in comfort and reliability — research operators carefully, especially for overnight routes.
Grab works well in Manila, Cebu City, and Davao — always use it instead of flagging unmarked taxis.
Tricycles (motorbike sidecars) are the main local transport in smaller towns — always agree on the price before boarding.
Jeepneys are cheap and iconic but routes can be confusing for first-time visitors — ask locals or use Google Maps for guidance.
Travel times between islands can be unpredictable due to weather, sea conditions, or schedule changes — always build in buffer days.

scams.name: Overpriced island tours
scams.description: Tour packages near popular beaches and ferry ports are often heavily inflated for tourists. Compare prices at multiple shops and ask your accommodation for recommendations.

scams.name: Tricycle overcharging
scams.description: Tricycle drivers near tourist areas frequently quote prices many times above the local rate. Always agree on a price before boarding and carry small bills.

scams.name: Tourist pricing in highly visited islands
scams.description: Restaurants, shops, and activities in places like Boracay, Coron, and El Nido often apply tourist pricing. Check online reviews for typical price ranges before buying.

scams.name: Fake ferry or tour operators
scams.description: Informal sellers near ports may pose as legitimate ferry or tour companies. Book through verified platforms like 12Go or Klook, or through your accommodation.

emergency.embassy_label: US Embassy in Manila / Consular assistance

embassy.phone: Verify official embassy contact before travel
embassy.address: Verify official embassy contact before travel

emergency.notes: Dial 911 for all emergencies in the Philippines. For emergencies involving US nationals, contact the US Embassy in Manila — verify contact details before travel.

checklist.item: Passport valid 6+ months beyond your intended departure date
Verify current 30-day visa exemption rules with the Bureau of Immigration Philippines or via the US Embassy in Manila
Purchase travel insurance (check diving, water activities, and medical evacuation coverage)
Book a return or onward ticket — required at immigration and may be checked at check-in
Notify your bank of travel dates to avoid card blocks
Download Google Maps offline and install Grab before arriving
Buy Airalo eSIM or arrange a local SIM on arrival at the airport
Bring USD cash — widely accepted in tourist areas and easy to exchange at money changers
Save Philippines emergency number (911) in your phone
Check typhoon season if traveling Jun–Oct — monitor PAGASA (Philippine weather agency)
Pack insect repellent — dengue is present throughout the Philippines
